  • Überblick
  • Baum
  • Index
  • Suchen
  • Hilfe

    Alle Klassen und Schnittstellen

    Klasse
    Beschreibung
    BoardManager
    Manages the state of the game board and interactions with it in the game.
    BoardScreenPanel
    This class creates the board screen panel for the game, which includes the game board, timer settings, and control buttons.
    CPUPlayer
    Represents a CPU player in the game, which operates based on a specific strategy.
    DifficultyLevel
    An enumeration representing different levels of difficulty.
    DifficultyScreenPanel
    This class creates the Difficulty Screen JPanel for the game.
    DifficultyStrategy
    The DifficultyStrategy class serves as an abstract representation of the decision-making logic for determining moves and removals in a game.
    EasyDifficulty
    Represents a strategy for the easy difficulty in the game.
    GameManager
    Manages the game flow, including player turns, moves, and game state.
    GameManager.LossCause
    Represents the various causes by which a player can lose the game.
    GameOverPhase
    Represents the game over phase in the game.
    GamePersistenceManager
    Manages the persistence of game states by saving and loading game data to and from JSON files.
    GamePhase
    This class represents the abstract base class for different gamePhases.
    GameStateData
    Represents a snapshot of the game state at a particular moment.
    GUI
    This class represents the main GUI of the game.
    GUIBoard
    This class is responsible for rendering and managing the graphical representation of the game board in a Nine Men's Morris game.
    GUIPrompt
    This class is responsible for updating the GUI prompts based on the current game state.
    HardDifficulty
    Represents a strategy for the hard difficulty in the game.
    History
    This class is responsible for managing the history of moves made in the game.
    HomeScreenPanel
    This class creates the Home Screen JPanel for the game.
    ImaginativePlayer
    ImaginativePlayer is a subclass of PlayerEntity that represents a player capable of planning moves based on the rules of the game without performing them.
    JumpingPhase
    This class represents the jumping phase of the game where players can jump to unoccupied nodes.
    MediumDifficulty
    Represents a strategy for the medium difficulty in the game.
    MenuBar
    This class creates the menu bar for the game, which includes a "Help" menu and a "Credits" menu.
    MenuScreenPanel
    This class creates the menu screen panel for the game, which includes buttons for starting a new game, loading a game, and returning to the home screen.
    Move
    Represents a snapshot of the game state at a particular moment.
    MoveData
    Represents a serializable snapshot of a move from the history.
    MovingPhase
    This class represents the moving phase of the game, where players can move their stones to neighbours nodes on the board.
    NewGameScreenPanel
    This class creates the New Game Screen JPanel.
    Node
    Represents a node in the game board.
    Observable
    Base class for observable objects in the Observer design pattern.
    Observer
    Interface for the Observer in the Observer design pattern.
    Player
    This class represents a human player in the game.
    PlayerData
    Represents a serializable snapshot of player data in the game.
    PlayerEntity
    This class represents the abstract base class for different players.
    RuleManager
    The RuleManager is responsible for checking the validity of moves and managing game rules.
    SettingPhase
    This class represents the setting phase of the game where players can place stones to unoccupied nodes.
    Stone
    Represents a stone in the game, which is owned by a player and placed on a node.
    StyleComponents
    This class is used to style different components in the GUI.
    TurnTimer
    This class implements a turn timer that counts down the time a player has to perform a move.
